title: Limnochloa acutangula
intro: Limnochloa acutangula, also known as Asian swamp grass or swamp saw-sedge, is a type of aquatic grass that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Limnochloa acutangula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Limnochloa acutangula is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Limnochloa acutangula is a type of grass, belonging to the family Poaceae. It is classified under the subfamily Chloridoideae. The genus Limnochloa is part of the tribe Cynodonteae. Limnochloa acutangula is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This species is often found growing in wetlands and grasslands.

preparations: Limnochloa acutangula is used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations are made from its leaves and roots.
